section_miscategorizations.text: The patient reports pain in their left elbow and left arm.
section_miscategorizations.reason: This is a patient-reported symptom and belongs in the Subjective section; placing it in Objective could mislead a reader into thinking it was an exam finding.

section_miscategorizations.text: The patient reports pain in their neck.
section_miscategorizations.reason: Neck pain here is clearly reported by the patient, not documented as a physical exam finding, so it should be in the Subjective section.

section_miscategorizations.text: The patient has a history of hypertension and diabetes.
section_miscategorizations.reason: Past medical history is typically documented in the Subjective portion; its presence in Assessment is more organizational than misleading but not ideal.
This is explicitly framed as a patient-reported symptom rather than the clinician's diagnostic impression, so it belongs in the Subjective section.
